name: Buffalo Transportation Inc
districts_or_customers: Erie County school districts via affiliated WNY Bus Co.
Maryvale School District (contested in 2024 v. First Student)
recent_news: 2024: Affiliated WNY Bus Co. defended a school transport contract from First Student's attempted monopolization (Maryvale district). 2017: Finkelshtein pleaded guilty to filing a false tax return; 2023 settlement of $91,838 with DOJ over false statements in COVID-19 funding applications.
fit_notes: Primarily NEMT, but Finkelshtein-controlled WNY Bus Co. provides yellow-bus/special-needs school transport in Erie County NY (a Summit expansion state). Concern: prior DOJ settlement and tax felony reduce attractiveness as a clean tuck-in. Small footprint (~40 employees here); deal would likely need to be structured around the school-bus affiliate.

name: Medicab Of Rochester
districts_or_customers: 3+ local Rochester-area school districts (historical wheelchair-student contracts)
NY State Office of Vocational Rehabilitation / Access VR
recent_news: No major M&A news identified. Long-standing local NEMT operator; fleet grew from 4 vehicles (1978) to ~50 by 2014.
fit_notes: 50-year-old family business led by surviving co-founder; founder died 2010, suggesting strong succession signal. Mix of NEMT and SPED/wheelchair school routes fits Summit's special-needs vertical in upstate NY (Summit footprint state). Smaller scale (~$2.4M revenue) — bolt-on for upstate NY platform rather than standalone.

name: Palmer Bus Svc
recent_news: Generational transition completed 2012 from Floyd/Lois Palmer to daughters Hollie Johnson and Jenna Fromm; Jenna Fromm assumed CEO role in 2015. Continues to expand across MN.
fit_notes: Highly attractive Midwest school-bus platform: 50+ years old, 22 MN divisions, recently transitioned to 2nd-gen sisters (low near-term succession pressure but possible private equity interest). Likely much larger than the ~$8M Austin-location figure suggests — full enterprise across 22 divisions could rival a regional platform acquisition vs. tuck-in for Summit in MN.

name: Dean Transportation
principals: Lyle L. Stephens (former leader, named company 1969)
districts_or_customers: Grand Rapids Public Schools and many MI districts
recent_news: 2019: Marked 50 years (as Special Transportation/Dean). Highlighted as one of MI's largest family-owned student transportation providers; continues to expand SPED expertise.
fit_notes: Statewide MI platform far larger than the $26M revenue figure suggests — ~1,700 vehicles, 2,400 employees, 46 locations, 80K students. Strong SPED franchise rooted in polio-era Lansing. Kellie Dean has led since 1986 (now ~40 yrs in), creating meaningful succession/exit consideration. Would be a platform-scale acquisition rather than tuck-in for Summit, but a marquee MI footprint.

name: Rohrer Bus Svc
principals: Skip Rohrer / Howard E. Rohrer III (President)
districts_or_customers: 15-20+ school districts in Central and Northeastern PA
Mid-Atlantic bus sales/service customers (PA, MD, NJ, DE, WV, VA)
recent_news: Celebrated ~100 years in business (founded ~1921). Continues as 4th-generation family-owned operator with multi-state dealer/service footprint. No public M&A activity identified.
fit_notes: Excellent PA tuck-in candidate (Summit expansion state): 4th-generation family business, ~100 years old, 15-20+ district contracts in Central/NE PA, ~1,200 buses/vans. Combined school-transport + Thomas/Wayne dealership creates complexity but a meaningful regional moat. Generational succession likely a relevant lever for diligence.
